planning_gate.spec_delta: M19 tests a support-health-gated knee-flexion micro-phase in the IK target family.

perspectives.product: directly tests whether the Unitree G1 model can express the remaining knee motion while retaining squat recovery
perspectives.architecture: patches EXP62.build_target and reuses the exp71 native evaluator/gates
perspectives.qa: native JSON per candidate plus visible 8cm, knee/hip, contact, slip, return gates
perspectives.skeptic: desired_fraction is reused during return, so this probe may need a descend-only evaluator if results are ambiguous

dod: run phase-windowed knee/hip micro-target candidates around the exp73/75 best timing
report whether achieved knee flexion exceeds the 0.513rad recoverable plateau without losing support/ZMP

sources.note: Task-based WBC combines CoM/ZMP regulation, feet pose tasks, and joint objectives for humanoid posture transitions.
Humanoid squatting is treated as whole-body coordination over a trajectory rather than an isolated joint target.
ZMP inside the support polygon is used as a balance condition for humanoid WBC.
Recent Unitree G1 work includes squat-to-height behavior and motivates explicit knee-flexion shaping.
